title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud  Firewall changes

input_row.title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud  Firewall changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Firewall rule changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for Create or Update firewall rule events gives insight network access changes and may reduce the time it takes to detect suspicious activity.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud Firewall Changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the Action Trail are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target actiontrail_log "event.serviceName": "Cloudfw" and ("event.eventName": CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy) | select count(1) as c
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up Cloud Firewall Changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. b. Check the Action Trail and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 5. Select Log Management > Actiontrail Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query "event.serviceName": "Cloudfw" and ("event.eventName": CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy) | select count(1) as c 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The provided audit approach includes detailed steps for evaluating log monitoring and alerting for Cloud Firewall changes through the SLS Console.
- The specific events related to the Cloud Firewall management can be monitored programmatically using APIs.
- The focus on Cloud products and logging services aligns with standard Alibaba Cloud capabilities that can be queried via APIs.
- Given the comprehensive detail in the description that implies the ability to set up specific queries for log monitoring, it indicates that this control can be validated using automation rather than relying on manual checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_sls_log_monitoring_cloud_firewall_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would check the following:
  - Use Alibaba Cloud APIs or Aliyun CLI SDK methods to confirm that alerts for specific Cloud Firewall events (CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy, DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy, ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy) are configured and enabled.
  - Verify that Action Trail is enabled in the respective logging service projects.
  - Command to check logs configuration: 
    - Use the appropriate API call or Aliyun CLI commands to check the settings in the SLS Log Service and verify the specific alert conditions.
  
- **Criteria for Validation:**
  1. Confirm that Action Trail is enabled:
     - Check the property for Action Trail state, ensuring it is 'enabled'.
  2. Validate the alert rule:
     - Validate that the alert conditions align with the specified events involving Cloud Firewall changes.
     - This would involve ensuring a query setup exists for the specific events related to Cloud Firewall which can be confirmed by querying the dashboard or logs.
     
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** 
    - Action Trail successfully enabled with relevant event monitoring.
    - Configured alert rule exists for any changes to Cloud Firewall policies.
  - **Fail:** 
    - Action Trail is disabled or not properly configured.
    - Missing or misconfigured alert rules for Cloud Firewall changes.

This automated approach leverages existing Alibaba Cloud logging capabilities, allowing for quick, consistent assessment of compliance with this security control.
